instruction: Pronounce the letter S sound clearly in isolation: /s/ like a snake hissing. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the short vowel A sound clearly in isolation: /ae/ like in apple and ant. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter T sound clearly in isolation: /t/ like tapping. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the short vowel I sound clearly in isolation: /ɪ/ like in it and in. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter P sound clearly in isolation: /p/ like popping. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter N sound clearly in isolation: /n/ like in no and nap. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter K/C sound clearly in isolation: /k/ like clicking. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the short vowel E sound clearly in isolation: /ɛ/ like in egg and end. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter H sound clearly in isolation: /h/ like breathing. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter R sound clearly in isolation: /r/ like in run and red. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter M sound clearly in isolation: /m/ like humming. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter D sound clearly in isolation: /d/ like a drum beat. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter G sound clearly in isolation: /g/ like in get and go. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the short vowel O sound clearly in isolation: /ɒ/ like in on and off. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the short vowel U sound clearly in isolation: /ʌ/ like in up and us. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter L sound clearly in isolation: /l/ like in lap and leg. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter F sound clearly in isolation: /f/ like air escaping a tire. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter B sound clearly in isolation: /b/ like bouncing. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph AI sound clearly: /eɪ/ like in rain and tail. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter J sound clearly in isolation: /dʒ/ like in jam and jet. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph OA sound clearly: /oʊ/ like in goat and boat. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph IE sound clearly: /aɪ/ like in tie and pie. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph EE sound clearly: /iː/ like in bee and tree. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph OR sound clearly: /ɔː/ like in corn and fork. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter Z sound clearly in isolation: /z/ like a buzzing bee. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter W sound clearly in isolation: /w/ like in win and web. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the digraph NG sound clearly: /ŋ/ like in ring and sing. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter V sound clearly in isolation: /v/ like vibrating. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the short OO sound clearly: /ʊ/ like in book and look. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the long OO sound clearly: /uː/ like in moon and food. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter Y sound clearly in isolation: /j/ like in yes and yam. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the letter X sound clearly in isolation: /ks/ like in fox and box. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the digraph CH sound clearly: /tʃ/ like a choo-choo train. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the digraph SH sound clearly: /ʃ/ like shushing someone quietly. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the voiced TH sound clearly: /ð/ like in the, this, that. Tongue between teeth, voice on. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the unvoiced TH sound clearly: /θ/ like in thin, three, think. Tongue between teeth, just air. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the digraph QU sound clearly: /kw/ like in queen and quick. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph OU sound clearly: /aʊ/ like in out and loud. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph OI sound clearly: /ɔɪ/ like in oil and coin. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph UE sound clearly: /juː/ like in blue and clue. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph ER sound clearly: /ɜː/ like in her and fern. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.
Pronounce the vowel digraph AR sound clearly: /ɑː/ like in car and star. American English phonics for children aged 4-8.

text: Oh no! Ants are crawling up your arm! A, a, a!
instruction: Natural American English storytelling for children. Warm, engaging narration. Clear and slow.

text: Watch the tennis ball go back and forth: t, t, t!

instruction: Natural American English storytelling for children. Warm, engaging narration. Clear and slow.

instruction: Natural American English storytelling for children. Warm, engaging narration. Clear and slow.
